completion_promises.criterion_text: All nine wrappers in src/forge/tools/guardkit.py, each decorated with @tool(parse_docstring=True)
completion_promises.evidence: src/forge/tools/guardkit.py defines exactly nine @tool(parse_docstring=True) async functions: guardkit_system_arch, guardkit_system_design, guardkit_system_plan, guardkit_feature_spec, guardkit_feature_plan, guardkit_task_review, guardkit_task_work, guardkit_task_complete, guardkit_autobuild. Verified at runtime: each wrapper.name matches the function name and wrapper.args_schema parses the docstring Args block. Tests: TestWrapperSurface.test_module_exports_all_nine_wrappers, TestWrapperSurface.test_wrapper_is_a_structured_tool (parametrised over all 9), TestWrapperSurface.test_args_schema_includes_repo_param.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Every wrapper returns a str (JSON-encoded GuardKitResult on success/failed/timeout, or {"status":"error","error":"..."} on internal exception per ADR-ARCH-025) — never raises
completion_promises.evidence: Each wrapper returns result.model_dump_json() (a str) on the happy path and _error_json(exc, tool_name) on the exception path. Tests verify isinstance(out, str) and json.loads(out) success across success/failed/timeout/exception scenarios. See TestSuccessJsonShape, TestFailedAndTimeoutPassThrough, TestInternalExceptionNeverRaises (parametrised over all 9 wrappers).

completion_promises.criterion_text: Function body of each wrapper is wrapped in try/except Exception as exc: returning the JSON error string
completion_promises.evidence: Every wrapper body has top-level try/except Exception that returns _error_json(exc, '<tool_name>'). The shared _invoke helper also has try/except so an exception path through the helper still folds to the error envelope. TestInternalExceptionNeverRaises parametrises a RuntimeError stub across all 9 wrappers; every assertion that decoded['status']=='error' passes.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Each wrapper calls forge.adapters.guardkit.run(subcommand=..., ...) with the right subcommand literal — no format / f-string command construction
completion_promises.evidence: Each wrapper passes a string literal to _invoke(subcommand=...) which forwards as a kwarg to guardkit_run(subcommand=...). The literals are 'system-arch', 'system-design', 'system-plan', 'feature-spec', 'feature-plan', 'task-review', 'task-work', 'task-complete', 'autobuild'. Verified by TestSubcommandLiteralWiring (parametrised over all 9), which captures the kwargs of the stub run() and asserts capture['subcommand'] equals the expected literal.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Each wrapper composes the NATS progress subscriber for telemetry; a missing subscriber must not fail the call
completion_promises.evidence: _invoke wraps the run() call in 'async with subscribe_progress(nats_client, build_id, subcommand, sink):'. With nats_client=None, subscribe_progress records a PROGRESS_STREAM_UNAVAILABLE warning on the sink and yields anyway — verified by TestProgressSubscriberComposition.test_missing_subscriber_does_not_fail_the_call (still returns success status). TestProgressSubscriberComposition.test_subscribe_progress_is_entered_for_each_call replaces subscribe_progress with a fake CM and asserts it was entered exactly once with the expected subcommand and a non-empty build_id.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Each wrapper logs via structlog with tool_name, duration_ms, status (per API-tool-layer.md §2)
completion_promises.evidence: _invoke logs one structured INFO line per call via the standard logging module: 'guardkit_tool_invocation tool_name=%s status=%s duration_ms=%d'. structlog is not a project dependency (not in pyproject.toml), but the key=value shape is preserved so downstream log processors can parse it identically. TestStructuredLogging.test_success_call_emits_log_with_tool_name_status_duration captures via caplog and asserts all three keys appear in the output for both success (status=success) and exception (status=error) paths.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Tools that take context_paths (only guardkit_feature_spec per §6.1) thread them through to run(extra_context_paths=...) for the explicit-context retry case
completion_promises.evidence: Only guardkit_feature_spec exposes a context_paths parameter (verified by TestContextPathsThreading.test_only_feature_spec_exposes_context_paths_param iterating every other wrapper's args_schema). guardkit_feature_spec passes context_paths into _invoke(extra_context_paths=context_paths), which forwards as guardkit_run(extra_context_paths=...). TestContextPathsThreading.test_feature_spec_threads_context_paths_to_extra_context_paths captures the exact list pass-through; the None case is also covered.

completion_promises.criterion_text: On a successful call, the returned JSON string contains artefacts, duration_secs, and coach_score when GuardKit produced one
completion_promises.evidence: result.model_dump_json() serialises the full GuardKitResult including artefacts (list, default []), duration_secs (required float), and coach_score (None when absent, float when present). TestSuccessJsonShape (parametrised over all 9 wrappers) parses the JSON and asserts decoded['artefacts'] non-empty, decoded['duration_secs'] >= 0, and decoded['coach_score'] == 0.92 against a fixture that supplies one.

completion_promises.criterion_text: PR creation is not in this file — that lives in a separate version_control tool layer (out of scope here)
completion_promises.evidence: src/forge/tools/guardkit.py contains only the nine guardkit_* wrappers and the shared _invoke / _error_json / _build_id_for helpers. No PR-creation logic, no imports from forge.adapters.gh, no version-control surface. Module __all__ enumerates exactly the nine wrappers.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Unit tests with a fake run(): each tool returns the right JSON shape on success/failed/timeout/exception; each tool's docstring parses correctly through @tool(parse_docstring=True) (verifiable by inspecting tool.args_schema)
completion_promises.evidence: tests/forge/tools/test_guardkit.py contains 75 tests organised in 9 classes. Parametrised tests cover all 9 wrappers across success, failed, timeout, and internal-exception paths. TestWrapperSurface inspects args_schema for every wrapper (verifying parse_docstring=True succeeded). All 75 tests pass: '75 passed, 1 warning in 0.29s'.

completion_promises.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors
completion_promises.evidence: Files follow the project code style (PEP 8, type hints, 88-char line length per .claude/rules/code-style.md, snake_case names, f-strings, pathlib.Path). No new dependencies. No bare except. No mutable module-level state. All imports use existing packages (langchain.tools, json, logging, time, pathlib, forge.adapters.guardkit.*). pyproject.toml does not configure black/ruff/mypy as gating tools, so the bar is the style guide and the test suite — both green.
